{"step_by_step_thinking": "According to the ACLS guidelines, defibrillation should be performed with the maximum available energy when indicated, which means it is not always necessary to use the maximum energy every time. Atropine is used for asystole, as stated in the guidelines. Immediate calling for help is recommended in both witnessed and unwitnessed cases. Cardiac massage resuscitation should be performed immediately after defibrillation without waiting for assessing the rhythm, as this is not aligned with the guidelines.", "answer_choice": "D"}
